title: American Literature Canon
description: Classic American novels, plays, and their authors

cards.front: Who wrote The Great Gatsby?
F. Scott Fitzgerald's novel about Jay Gatsby and the Jazz Age on Long Island
Who wrote The Scarlet Letter?
Nathaniel Hawthorne's novel about Hester Prynne in Puritan Massachusetts
Who wrote The Grapes of Wrath?
John Steinbeck's novel about the Joad family migrating to California during the Dust Bowl
Who wrote The Catcher in the Rye?
J.D. Salinger's novel narrated by Holden Caulfield
Who wrote To Kill a Mockingbird?
Harper Lee's novel set in Maycomb, Alabama, featuring Atticus Finch
Who wrote Fahrenheit 451?
Ray Bradbury's dystopian novel about a future society that burns books
Who wrote Of Mice and Men?
John Steinbeck's novella about George and Lennie, two migrant workers in California
Who wrote Moby-Dick?
Herman Melville's novel about Captain Ahab's obsessive hunt for a white whale
Who wrote Adventures of Huckleberry Finn?
Mark Twain's novel about a boy and Jim rafting down the Mississippi River
Who wrote The Adventures of Tom Sawyer?
Mark Twain's novel about a mischievous boy in the fictional town of St. Petersburg, Missouri
Who wrote Catch-22?
Joseph Heller's satirical World War II novel set on a Mediterranean island
Who wrote The Crucible?
Arthur Miller's play about the Salem witch trials, written as an allegory for McCarthyism
Who wrote Death of a Salesman?
Arthur Miller's play about Willy Loman, a failing traveling salesman
Who wrote A Streetcar Named Desire?
Tennessee Williams' play featuring Blanche DuBois and Stanley Kowalski in New Orleans
Who wrote Our Town?
Thornton Wilder's play set in the fictional town of Grover's Corners, New Hampshire
Who wrote The Old Man and the Sea?
Ernest Hemingway's novella about an aging Cuban fisherman battling a giant marlin
cards.back: The Old Man and the Sea

cards.front: Who wrote Beloved?
Toni Morrison's novel about a formerly enslaved woman haunted by her past, set after the Civil War
Who wrote The Color Purple?
Alice Walker's epistolary novel about Celie, an African American woman in the rural South
Who wrote Invisible Man?
Ralph Ellison's novel about an unnamed Black man exploring identity and racism in America
Who wrote Their Eyes Were Watching God?
Zora Neale Hurston's novel about Janie Crawford's search for love and independence in Florida
Who wrote Slaughterhouse-Five?
Kurt Vonnegut's anti-war novel featuring Billy Pilgrim, who becomes 'unstuck in time'
Who wrote The Glass Menagerie?
Tennessee Williams' memory play about the Wingfield family, narrated by Tom
Who wrote A Raisin in the Sun?
Lorraine Hansberry's play about the Younger family's dreams on Chicago's South Side
Who wrote I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings?
Maya Angelou's autobiography about growing up in the segregated South
cards.back: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings

cards.front: Who wrote A Farewell to Arms?
Ernest Hemingway's novel about an American ambulance driver in World War I Italy

Who wrote The House of the Seven Gables?
Who wrote East of Eden?
John Steinbeck's novel retelling the Cain and Abel story in California's Salinas Valley
Who wrote The Sun Also Rises?
Ernest Hemingway's novel about disillusioned expatriates in Europe after World War I
Who wrote Song of Solomon?
Who wrote The Bluest Eye?
In Death of a Salesman, what is the main character's full name?
In A Streetcar Named Desire, who says "I have always depended on the kindness of strangers"?
What literary period is The Great Gatsby associated with?
What war does Slaughterhouse-Five center around, including the bombing of Dresden?
In Fahrenheit 451, what is the temperature at which book paper catches fire?
In Fahrenheit 451, what is the main character's name?
